findings: Regular rhythm with normal P waves preceding each QRS. Rate approximately 80 bpm. Extreme left axis deviation. Prolonged PR interval beyond 200 ms with every P conducted. RSR' pattern in V1 with QRS under 120 ms (requires clinician confirmation). Left axis deviation with small q in aVL and rS in the inferior leads (requires clinician confirmation). Pathological Q waves in the anteroseptal (V1, V2, and V3) leads.
impression: Findings consistent with sinus rhythm. First-degree AV block. Incomplete right bundle branch block. Left anterior fascicular block. Anteroseptal myocardial infarction.
